title: ophrys tenthredinifera subsp. neglecta
intro: Ophrys tenthredinifera subsp. neglecta, also known as the 'Wasp Orchid', is a rare subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the genus Ophrys. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits associated with the Wasp Orchid include relief from pain and swelling, as well as potential antimicrobial and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots or rhizomes are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
